name: explain_code
description: Produces a natural language explanation of the provided source code at a configurable level of detail. Can focus the explanation on specific aspects such as performance characteristics, security concerns, or stylistic quality.

code.description: The source code snippet to be explained.
examples:
def binary_search(arr, target):
    lo, hi = 0, len(arr)-1
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = (lo+hi)//2
        if arr[mid] == target: return mid
        elif arr[mid] < target: lo = mid+1
        else: hi = mid-1
    return -1
SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id) FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.name HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 5
const memoize = fn => { const cache = {}; return (...args) => { const key = JSON.stringify(args); return key in cache ? cache[key] : (cache[key] = fn(...args)); }; };
code.$comment: No language detection is done automatically; always pair with the language field for accurate explanation.

language.description: The programming language or query language of the submitted code. Used to tailor syntax-aware explanations.
language.$comment: Free-form string (not enum) to support a wider range of languages (SQL, Bash, Kotlin, etc.) beyond what lint_code supports.

detail_level.description: Controls the depth and verbosity of the generated explanation.
detail_level.$comment: 'brief' returns a 1-3 sentence summary; 'standard' explains logic block by block; 'detailed' includes line-by-line breakdown and edge case discussion.

focus.description: Optional lens to focus the explanation on a particular aspect of the code quality or characteristics.
focus.$comment: 'overall' provides a balanced explanation; specialized foci direct attention to complexity, vulnerabilities, or coding conventions respectively.
